required_check: Count total delivered files and classify usable vs ignored files
acceptance_criteria: Total count, usable count, ignored count, and error count are recorded

required_check: Identify PDF, DOCX, HTML, XML, TXT, CSV, JSON, ZIP, image, or unknown formats
acceptance_criteria: Every file has a detected format and parser route or quarantine reason

required_check: Detect text encoding and decode confidence for text-like files
acceptance_criteria: All text files decode as UTF-8 or have conversion record; undecodable files quarantined
dry_run_method: Read-only decode sample and error count

required_check: Generate dedupe keys from raw_sha256 and canonical case identity
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate raw files and duplicate case keys are reported separately
dry_run_method: Hash all raw inputs; derive canonical_case_key where fields exist

required_check: Extract court, chamber, and decision date
acceptance_criteria: Sample precision is manually checked; unknown fields remain explicit unknown, not inferred
dry_run_method: Parser dry run plus sampled review packet

required_check: Extract case_no and decision_no from headings/body
acceptance_criteria: Raw and normalized values are retained; collisions are reported
dry_run_method: Regex/parser dry run with collision table

required_check: Extract judicial citation labels and publication source markers
acceptance_criteria: Citation label must be reconstructable from metadata; unsupported labels flagged

required_check: Extract statute_refs and article_refs from decision text
acceptance_criteria: References are linked when possible; ambiguous refs remain unresolved, not guessed
dry_run_method: NER/regex dry run with sampled legal review

required_check: Assess personal data, party names, addresses, national IDs, financial or sensitive case data
acceptance_criteria: Risk bands and redaction requirements are recorded before sharing or indexing
dry_run_method: PII scan dry run on samples and high-risk files

required_check: Measure character/token/page distribution and extreme outliers
acceptance_criteria: Chunking strategy is based on distribution; outliers have parser or OCR notes

required_check: Detect scanned/image-only PDFs and OCR quality risk
acceptance_criteria: OCR-required files are counted; OCR engine and quality threshold selected before text extraction acceptance
dry_run_method: PDF text-layer audit and image ratio sample

required_check: Compute raw_sha256 for original files and parsed_text_sha256 for extracted text
acceptance_criteria: Hashes are stable and recorded before any transformation-derived artifact is trusted

required_check: Define chunk boundaries that preserve case identity and legal reasoning context
acceptance_criteria: No chunk loses canonical_case_key, citation_label, court/chamber/date, or source file hash

required_check: Design separate judicial collection/lane; do not merge into mevzuat
acceptance_criteria: Index plan names judicial collection, metadata fields, embedding model, and rollback/no-op plan
dry_run_method: Plan artifact only; no production index creation

required_check: Create stratified legal review sample before large-scale retrieval validation
acceptance_criteria: Sample covers courts, chambers, years, topics, formats, OCR/non-OCR, and duplicate cases
dry_run_method: Sampling dry run from inventory only
